prompt: Is there a relationship between course credits and average grade?

If you want the relationship within a specific semester, department, or course level, add filters or additional group-by dimensions.","If component scores are on different scales or need weighting (e.g., exam 70%, project 20%, attendance 10%), the numeric-grade expression must be adjusted accordingly.","We include all enrollments that have at least one numeric component; we do not further filter by enrollments.is_active, academic_year, or semester_type. Add filters if you need to restrict to active enrollments or a time window."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits
ORDER BY c.credits ASC;","validationIssues":["All encoded fields (credits, avg_grade, n_enrollments) are present in metadata.columns — good.","SQL groups by c.credits so there will be one aggregated point per credits value; with few distinct credit values the scatter will be discrete and may hide within-credit variability (semester/course-level effects).","The y-axis domain is fixed to [0,100] in the visualization; this assumes component scores and the computed avg_grade are on a 0–100 scale — if scores use a different scale, the domain could be misleading.","The SQL assumes equal weighting of available numeric components when computing per-enrollment grade; if components should be weighted (e.g., exam 70%, project 20%, attendance 10%) the calculation and resulting chart would change.","Color and size both encode n_enrollments (redundant encodings). This is not invalid but may be unnecessary; consider using one channel for clarity."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Compute each enrollment's numeric grade as the mean of its non-null numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score), then aggregate by courses.credits to produce average grade and enrollment counts. Joins follow confirmed foreign keys: courses -> course_sections -> enrollments -> grades. Filtered out grade rows with no numeric components. Results ordered by credits for determinism.","sql":"SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits
ORDER BY c.credits ASC;","assumptions":["Per-enrollment numeric grade is the simple mean of the non-null numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score).","grades.enrollment_id is NOT NULL and grades has at most one row per enrollment (confirmed by unique index).","Aggregation collapses across courses/sections/semesters as requested (one row per integer credits value).","Do not use grades.grade_value because its user-defined type/encoding wasn't mapped; only numeric component columns are used."]},"execution":{"ok":true,"sql":"SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits

Results ordered by credits for determinism.","sql":"SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits
ORDER BY c.credits ASC;","assumptions":["Per-enrollment numeric grade is the simple mean of the non-null numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score).","grades.enrollment_id is NOT NULL and grades has at most one row per enrollment (confirmed by unique index).","Aggregation collapses across courses/sections/semesters as requested (one row per integer credits value).","Do not use grades.grade_value because its user-defined type/encoding wasn't mapped; only numeric component columns are used."]},"execution":{"ok":true,"sql":"SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id

visualization.title: Average numeric grade vs course credits

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits
ORDER BY c.credits ASC;

issues: All encoded fields (credits, avg_grade, n_enrollments) are present in metadata.columns — good.

SQL groups by c.credits so there will be one aggregated point per credits value; with few distinct credit values the scatter will be discrete and may hide within-credit variability (semester/course-level effects).

The y-axis domain is fixed to [0,100] in the visualization; this assumes component scores and the computed avg_grade are on a 0–100 scale — if scores use a different scale, the domain could be misleading.

The SQL assumes equal weighting of available numeric components when computing per-enrollment grade; if components should be weighted (e.g., exam 70%, project 20%, attendance 10%) the calculation and resulting chart would change.
Color and size both encode n_enrollments (redundant encodings). This is not invalid but may be unnecessary; consider using one channel for clarity.

visualization.title: Average numeric grade vs course credits

result.sql:
SELECT c.credits AS credits,
       AVG((COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
           / NULLIF((CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
                     + CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0))
           AS avg_grade,
       COUNT(e.id) AS n_enrollments
FROM public.courses AS c
JOIN public.course_sections AS cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
JOIN public.enrollments AS e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
JOIN public.grades AS g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
WHERE (g.exam_score IS NOT NULL OR g.project_score IS NOT NULL OR g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY c.credits
ORDER BY c.credits ASC;

issues: All encoded fields (credits, avg_grade, n_enrollments) are present in metadata.columns — good.

The y-axis domain is fixed to [0,100] in the visualization; this assumes component scores and the computed avg_grade are on a 0–100 scale — if scores use a different scale, the domain could be misleading.

The SQL assumes equal weighting of available numeric components when computing per-enrollment grade; if components should be weighted (e.g., exam 70%, project 20%, attendance 10%) the calculation and resulting chart would change.
Color and size both encode n_enrollments (redundant encodings). This is not invalid but may be unnecessary; consider using one channel for clarity.
